title: GitHub Review by greptile-apps[bot]
summary:
The reviewer identified three bugs and one improvement:

1. **Scope ConfigMap name to CR name**: The immutable ConfigMap uses a hardcoded `"keystone-config"` base name instead of `fmt.Sprintf("%s-config", keystone.Name)`, which breaks multi-instance deployments in the same namespace due to owner-UID conflicts.

2. **Pass `maxActiveKeys` to the fernet rotation CronJob**: The CronJob doesn't mount `keystone.conf`, so `fernet_rotate` uses oslo.config's default of 3 keys instead of `spec.fernet.maxActiveKeys`, silently pruning active keys and causing HTTP 401s. Fix by injecting an `OS_fernet_tokens__max_active_keys` env var.

3. **Align MariaDB Database/Grant names with `spec.database.database`**: The Database CR and Grant use `keystone.Name` while the connection URL uses `spec.database.database`, so when they differ the db_sync Job and API get `Access Denied`. Both `buildDatabase` and `buildGrant` should use `spec.database.database`. Additionally, `resolveCacheServers` hardcodes 3 Memcached endpoints regardless of the referenced cluster's actual replica count â€” the reviewer suggests deriving the count dynamically or documenting the constraint.

code_comments.body:
**Immutable ConfigMap base name not scoped to CR name**

`CreateImmutableConfigMap` is called with the hardcoded base name `"keystone-config"` rather than `fmt.Sprintf("%s-config", keystone.Name)`. When two `Keystone` CRs in the same namespace produce a ConfigMap with the same content (identical configs, same hash suffix), the second CR's reconciler will hit the owner-UID check inside `CreateImmutableConfigMap` and return:

```
existing ConfigMap <ns>/keystone-config-<hash> is not owned by <ns>/<second-cr-name>
```

This permanently blocks the second CR's reconcile chain. Even when configs differ (different hashes), both CRs emit ConfigMaps with the `keystone-config-` prefix, making it ambiguous in a cluster which ConfigMap belongs to which instance.

The fix mirrors the pattern used for every other managed resource in this PR:

```suggestion
	configMapName, err := config.CreateImmutableConfigMap(ctx, r.Client, r.Scheme, keystone,
		fmt.Sprintf("%s-config", keystone.Name), keystone.Namespace, data)
```

<details><summary>Prompt To Fix With AI</summary>

`````markdown

Line: 143-144

When two `Keystone` CRs in the same namespace produce a ConfigMap with the same content (identical configs, same hash suffix), the second CR's reconciler will hit the owner-UID check inside `CreateImmutableConfigMap` and return:

```
existing ConfigMap <ns>/keystone-config-<hash> is not owned by <ns>/<second-cr-name>
```

This permanently blocks the second CR's reconcile chain. Even when configs differ (different hashes), both CRs emit ConfigMaps with the `keystone-config-` prefix, making it ambiguous in a cluster which ConfigMap belongs to which instance.

The fix mirrors the pattern used for every other managed resource in this PR:

```suggestion
	configMapName, err := config.CreateImmutableConfigMap(ctx, r.Client, r.Scheme, keystone,
		fmt.Sprintf("%s-config", keystone.Name), keystone.Namespace, data)
```

How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise.
`````

</details>
code_comments.author: greptile-apps[bot]

code_comments.body:
**Rotation CronJob ignores `spec.fernet.maxActiveKeys` â€” may silently prune active keys**

`fernetRotationCronJob` builds the CronJob without a `keystone.conf` volume mount, so `keystone-manage fernet_rotate` resolves `fernet_tokens.max_active_keys` from oslo.config's compiled-in default (3). If a user sets `spec.fernet.maxActiveKeys` to a value greater than 3, the CronJob will discard the newer staging key on every rotation instead of keeping it, shrinking the active key window.

Once a token has been signed with a key that the rotation job has pruned, Keystone returns HTTP 401 for that token â€” even while the pod's in-memory key list (loaded from the fernet-keys Secret) still includes it.

`fernetRotationCronJob` does not yet accept a `configMapName` argument because it runs before `reconcileConfig` in the current sequence. The simplest correct fix is to pass `maxActiveKeys` directly via an environment variable:

```go
Env: []corev1.EnvVar{
    // ...existing SECRET_NAME / SECRET_NAMESPACE entries...
    {Name: "OS_fernet_tokens__max_active_keys",
     Value: strconv.Itoa(int(keystone.Spec.Fernet.MaxActiveKeys))},
},
```

oslo.config honours `OS_<GROUP>__<KEY>` environment variable overrides, so this value will take precedence over the config-file default without needing to mount the ConfigMap.

<details><summary>Prompt To Fix With AI</summary>

`````markdown

If a user sets `spec.fernet.maxActiveKeys` to a value greater than 3, the CronJob will discard the newer staging key on every rotation instead of keeping it, shrinking the active key window.

Once a token has been signed with a key that the rotation job has pruned, Keystone returns HTTP 401 for that token â€” even while the pod's in-memory key list (loaded from the fernet-keys Secret) still includes it.

`fernetRotationCronJob` does not yet accept a `configMapName` argument because it runs before `reconcileConfig` in the current sequence. The simplest correct fix is to pass `maxActiveKeys` directly via an environment variable:

```go
Env: []corev1.EnvVar{
    // ...existing SECRET_NAME / SECRET_NAMESPACE entries...
    {Name: "OS_fernet_tokens__max_active_keys",
     Value: strconv.Itoa(int(keystone.Spec.Fernet.MaxActiveKeys))},
},
```

oslo.config honours `OS_<GROUP>__<KEY>` environment variable overrides, so this value will take precedence over the config-file default without needing to mount the ConfigMap.

How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise.
`````

code_comments.author: greptile-apps[bot]

code_comments.body:
**MariaDB Database name diverges from `spec.database.database` â€” Grant and connection URL target different databases**

`buildDatabase` creates the `mariadbv1alpha1.Database` CR with `Name: keystone.Name` and no explicit `Spec.Name` field. The mariadb-operator defaults the actual database name inside MariaDB to the CR's `metadata.name`, i.e. `keystone.Name`.

`buildGrant` then grants `ALL PRIVILEGES` on `Database: keystone.Name` to `Username: keystone.Name`.

However, `reconcileConfig` constructs the SQLAlchemy connection URL using:
```go
Path: keystone.Spec.Database.Database,
```

So the connection URL points to database `spec.database.database` while the MariaDB CR and the Grant operate on database `keystone.Name`. When these two values differ (e.g. a CR named `keystone-prod` with `spec.database.database: keystone`), Keystone connects to a database that:
1. Does not exist (the MariaDB operator created `keystone-prod`, not `keystone`)
2. Has no user grant for the configured credentials

Both the db_sync Job and runtime API calls will fail with `1045 Access Denied`.

The MariaDB `Database` CR spec should be aligned with `spec.database.database`. Check whether the mariadb-operator's `DatabaseSpec` has an explicit `Name` field (many versions do). If so, set it:

```go
Spec: mariadbv1alpha1.DatabaseSpec{
    // ...
    Name: &keystone.Spec.Database.Database,
},
```

`buildGrant` must also use `keystone.Spec.Database.Database`:

```go
Database: keystone.Spec.Database.Database,
```

<details><summary>Prompt To Fix With AI</summary>

`````markdown

Line: 103-160

`buildGrant` then grants `ALL PRIVILEGES` on `Database: keystone.Name` to `Username: keystone.Name`.

However, `reconcileConfig` constructs the SQLAlchemy connection URL using:
```go
Path: keystone.Spec.Database.Database,
```

So the connection URL points to database `spec.database.database` while the MariaDB CR and the Grant operate on database `keystone.Name`. When these two values differ (e.g. a CR named `keystone-prod` with `spec.database.database: keystone`), Keystone connects to a database that:
1. Does not exist (the MariaDB operator created `keystone-prod`, not `keystone`)
2. Has no user grant for the configured credentials

Both the db_sync Job and runtime API calls will fail with `1045 Access Denied`.

The MariaDB `Database` CR spec should be aligned with `spec.database.database`. Check whether the mariadb-operator's `DatabaseSpec` has an explicit `Name` field (many versions do). If so, set it:

```go
Spec: mariadbv1alpha1.DatabaseSpec{
    // ...
    Name: &keystone.Spec.Database.Database,
},
```

`buildGrant` must also use `keystone.Spec.Database.Database`:

```go
Database: keystone.Spec.Database.Database,
```

How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise.
`````
</details>
code_comments.author: greptile-apps[bot]

code_comments.body:
**`resolveCacheServers` always generates exactly 3 Memcached endpoints**

The managed-mode cache helper hard-codes pod ordinals `0`, `1`, `2` regardless of `spec.cache.clusterRef`'s actual replica count:

```go
return fmt.Sprintf("memcached-0.%s:11211,memcached-1.%s:11211,memcached-2.%s:11211", name, name, name)
```

If the referenced Memcached StatefulSet is deployed with 1 or 2 replicas (e.g. in a dev environment), Keystone's oslo.cache driver will include unreachable endpoints in the pymemcache pool. With 4 or more replicas, the extra pods are silently excluded from the cache ring, reducing effective cache capacity.

Consider exposing a `replicas` field on `CacheSpec`, or deriving the endpoint list from the number of replicas configured in the referenced cluster, or at minimum documenting that `spec.cache.clusterRef` requires exactly 3 replicas.

<details><summary>Prompt To Fix With AI</summary>

`````markdown

```

If the referenced Memcached StatefulSet is deployed with 1 or 2 replicas (e.g. in a dev environment), Keystone's oslo.cache driver will include unreachable endpoints in the pymemcache pool. With 4 or more replicas, the extra pods are silently excluded from the cache ring, reducing effective cache capacity.

Consider exposing a `replicas` field on `CacheSpec`, or deriving the endpoint list from the number of replicas configured in the referenced cluster, or at minimum documenting that `spec.cache.clusterRef` requires exactly 3 replicas.

How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise.
`````
